activities_projects.title: Analyzing the Symbolism of Shiuli Flowers
activities_projects.objective: Understand the use of natural elements as symbols of hope and time
steps: Read the lines mentioning the Shiuli flowers (morning jasmine).
Research why these flowers are significant in the morning (they bloom at night and fall at dawn).
Write a 60-word paragraph on why the child hopes these 'blooms of the dawn' will be carried safely to land in the night.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the use of imagery and symbolism in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of symbolic interpretation and clarity of expression (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Personification in the Sky
activities_projects.objective: Identify and explain the personification of clouds
steps: Find the stanza where the child looks up at the sky and sees clouds.
Identify how the 'little clouds' are personified (e.g., setting their white bulging sails).
Explain in 3 sentences how this personification makes the sky feel like a companion to the child's play.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify and analyze the effect of personification.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and logical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Playmate in the Sky
activities_projects.objective: Examine the child's innocent view of competition and play
steps: Identify who the child thinks is sending the clouds down to race with his boats.
Discuss in a small group: Is the 'playmate' real or imaginary?
Write a short dialogue (5 lines) between the child and the sky.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explore the theme of imagination and childhood innocence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and thematic consistency in the dialogue (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Archaic and Poetic Terms
steps: Find the words 'Bulging', 'Laden', 'Fairies', and 'Launch' in the poem.
Write a synonym for each that maintains the poetic tone.
Use 'Laden' in a sentence about a tree full of fruit.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct synonym selection and sentence construction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Journey of the 'Unknown'
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the theme of the 'strange land' and 'unknown friend'
steps: Why does the child write his name and address so clearly?
Analyze the phrase 'some stranger in some strange land'. What does this tell us about the child's curiosity about the world?
List three adjectives that describe the child's feelings toward the world outside.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze the theme of human connection and global curiosity.
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness of the analysis and use of adjectives (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Grammar: Participles as Adjectives
activities_projects.objective: Identify and use present participles in descriptions
steps: Find '-ing' words in the poem used as adjectives (e.g., 'running stream', 'bulging sails').
Create a table showing the Noun being described by the Participle.
Write two original sentences using participles to describe a 'rainy day'.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students master the use of participles in descriptive writing.
activities_projects.evaluation: Grammatical accuracy and correct identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Extension: The Fairies' Perspective
activities_projects.objective: Practice narrative writing from an alternative point of view
steps: The poem ends with the 'fairies of sleep' sailing in the boats.
Write an 80-word story about where the boats go once the child falls asleep.
Include sensory details like the sound of the water and the scent of the flowers.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative voice and sensory description skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, flow, and use of sensory imagery (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tone and Mood Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify the shift from reality to dream in the poem
steps: Highlight lines that describe real actions in one color and dream-like events in another.
Explain in 2 sentences how the 'mood' changes as the poem progresses from day to night.
Find one word in the text that signals this shift (e.g., 'night', 'sleep', 'dream').
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize shifts in literary mood and tone.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct categorization and clarity of mood analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Comparison: Boats vs. Clouds
activities_projects.objective: Contrast the physical world with the celestial world
steps: Make a T-chart comparing the 'Paper Boats' and the 'Clouds'.
Consider their color, who 'launches' them, and where they travel.
Summarize how the child connects the earth and the sky through his play.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice comparative analysis in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of the chart and quality of the summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: A Letter to Tagore
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on the universal appeal of the poem's theme
steps: Write a formal letter to the poet, Rabindranath Tagore.
Explain which image from the poem you found most beautiful and why.
Ask one question about why he chose 'paper boats' as the subject of the poem.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice formal letter writing and personal reflection.
activities_projects.evaluation: Letter format, sincerity of reflection, and engagement with the text (10 marks)
